response.justification: James Smith is an experienced full-stack and systems software developer with a strong background in web platforms, low-latency services, and developer tooling, but has no discernible overlap with the core requirements of a Principal Performance Architect focused on AI accelerator hardware. There is no evidence of SoC or IP modeling, GPU or SIMD/MIMD architecture work, deep-learning workload analysis, or hardware-software co-design. The mention of 'AI-agent infrastructure' at re:cinq refers to software-level agentic workflows, not hardware optimization. The role demands a fundamentally different technical discipline than the candidate's entire career trajectory.
strengths: Demonstrated performance engineering experience in high-throughput, low-latency environments (Dunnhumby: millions of HTTP req/s, async I/O, fiber-based concurrency)
Broad systems programming exposure including C/C++, embedded systems, and DLang, indicating comfort below the application layer
Over 14 years of professional software experience, meeting the bachelor's-degree experience threshold on paper
concerns: Zero experience with SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD pipelines, or any hardware performance analysis — the entire technical core of the role
No background in deep learning, neural network training/inference optimization, or AI accelerator workloads of any kind
Career trajectory is exclusively web, mobile, and cloud-native software; no evidence of hardware-software co-design or system architecture at the silicon or firmware level

key_factors.factor: No AI/ML workload or accelerator background
